name: Alexander, R. U.
motto: Lead me to it.
description: Here's the guy that caught the 'Little Bird.' He doesn't know the meaning of the word 'Stop!' although tales to the contrary have been circulated. As a letter writer he is unsurpassed--references: see 'That Dirty Fant Boy.' 'ALEX' is a jolly good fellow, and the college honors bestowed upon him are merely indications of the esteem and respect that his fellow students have for him. 'The City of Seven Hills' is patiently awaiting the return of this man who does things.

name: Anderson, Joseph M.
motto: The greatest pleasure of life is love
description: When this abbreviated, one-hundred-pound monster arrived at Clemson, the question naturally arose 'How did it get loose?' However, as time has 'fugited,' we have found that he had his teeth, and could talk as plainly as the rest of us. For a while those 'spy-glasses set up on sticks' attracted him, but he soon decided that they were too heavy to carry. His veterinary training will land him a job in some philanthropist's pet hospital.

name: Bull
motto: Size is not everything
clubs: Annual Celebration Y. M. C. A.
description: is very fond of hunting. Once, when on a hunt, he tracked a 'Tiger' to its lair, among a vast assemblage of buildings. Being tired, he rested, to get his game next day. However, he was arrested for hunting on the Reservation, and being arraigned before the Chief of the Tribe he was sentenced five years for the offense. This was in 1908; now he's proud to say that 1913 will see him marching, with his 'dip,' to make Mount Pleasant the 'Garden Spot of the World.'

name: Dingle, Bull
motto: Laugh, and the world laughs with you
description: When DINGLE decided to give the world his services, he chose Clemson as the first unlucky place to be startled. He has tried hard, but no one takes him seriously, because he imitates "Judge," "Puck," and "Life." His military genius never blossomed, but his literary talent is fully recognized. As a pigskin warrior, size, or rather lack of it, was his only drawback. We hope some day to see him Editor of the "Cherry's Clarion."

name: Aiken, Joseph
motto: Ye gods, but annihilate time, and make two mortals happy.
description: He came to Clemson five years ago, but fondness for 'trig' and unlucky numbers caused him to cast his lot with the Class of '13. Seldom speaking, 'Pete' seldom fails to say something when he does. Dancing, pretty ladies, and poetry are weaknesses; and we predict a career as a society butterfly.

name: Skinner, Crip.
motto: My performances have been stupendous
description: Four years ago, "Skinner" left Hyman to enter Clemson. He was determined to conquer the Electrical course, and but for "Crip." "Sam," and a few more would have found the course easy. "Skinner" is a devoted student, and practices as well as preaches the Rules and Regulations. He is steady and quiet, and, like the body in the Law of Inertia, he will move in a straight line unless acted on by some external force.

name: Dameron, Douglas
motto: Cannon to the right of them, Cannon to the left of them, Volleyed and thundered.
description: This blonde youth is commonly called "Doug," but is often referred to as "Clemson's Cannon." He was wheeled over from the "Spartan City," and "unlimbered," in '09, and since that time has done some effective firing in winning for himself the respect and admiration of both faculty and students. His most destructive projectiles seem to be his numerous "love epistles," which have found targets and scored "hits" at such distant ranges as Florida and Massachusetts. Nothing but success can await guns of his caliber.

name: Charleston, Joe
motto: I will write mine own epitaph; 'Heads up!' is good enough for me
description: "Joe" is an ardent advocate of the present military system, brought about by the New Regime. He always keeps in mind the maxim of "Hobo," and never hesitates to "bear down." He is always on the job, and his strict sense of honor and duty will bring him success in his future life.

name: Milton
motto: A beautiful face is a silent commendation
description: Milton is a quiet, good-natured guy, with a strong liking for rest. When there is any work to be done, he is always ready to help—get someone else to do it. His specialty is fooling the professors into thinking he knows a lot. His electrical dreams, if accomplished, would make this earth a heaven. We predict for him a great future as manager of the Courtenay telephone system.

name: Lancaster, Roy
description: "Roy," "Slim Jim," and "Fatty" are a few of his handles. After several years' preparatory training at S. C. C. I. University (?), "Grub" entered Fresh at Clemson. From the very outset his native ability was apparent. At the store he ate everybody "under the table." In fact, he ate so much that upon coming out again into the cooler air he invariably got "chilly." Although enjoying the Senior privileges of the campus, "Roy" spends his nights in hard study in his room.

name: Pickens, Hobo
motto: Shall we that nature blind, Checked her hand and changed her mind, Just when she had exactly wrought A finished pattern without fault?
description: He came from Liberty, sadly and sorrowfully, but fully settled on overcoming that "13" jinx. Thinking the road of the 'Weary Willies' the easiest, he joined the 'hoboes,' and has since been a quiet, unpretentious member of that clan. "Jesse" does not include in his military catalog, reveille, for he holds that a lieutenant deserves some privileges. Football interested him, but the faculty decided his life was too valuable to be risked. He's determined to become a ticket collector on the Blue Ridge.

name: Hobby, J. All.
motto: He never gets up to see, If there is any reveille.
description: "Hobby," after trying his fate at B. S. H. S. and Wofford—where he studied for the ministry—came to Clemson. His military aspirations are high; he once held a "P. D.," and never cut reveille. His favorite pastime is going to church on Sunday. "Hobby" never worries, but when there is work to be done he always manages to escape unharmed. We predict for this man a great future in his chosen profession, and the "City of Success" will some day be proud of her son.

name: Evans, Paul William
motto: When I ope' my mouth, let no dog bark
description: "Bousie," who hails from the "City by the Sea"—and well may she be proud of him—came to Clemson in the fall of '09. He cast his lot with the Chemists, and has shown by his work to be a man of ability and intellect. He is very military, as the length of the steps along the walk to the Postoffice will show, and if he doesn't become the superintendent of a Chinese Sunday School, will be a great chemist.

name: Kershaw, Bowser
motto: Hang sorrow; care will kill a cat; And therefore let's be merry
description: Bowser hails from the sandhills, Kershaw being the particular hill which he calls home. He amuses himself by engaging in all forms of gymnastics, including the flinging of ink on sheets of paper, which last he calls letter-writing. His most prominent characteristic is his laughter, which may be heard from the barracks to the Horticultural Grounds. When not otherwise engaged, he spends his time in Greenville. Although he has some gloomy days, we feel sure that the future will bring him success as a chemist or lawyer.

name: Barnwell
motto: Let me drill my company—I don't care for pie
description: Twas a big, clear-eyed youth of sixteen that left the swamps of Blackville, in 1909, to enter Clemson, and find another use for the swamps than as a place in which to hunt. He took the right track when he decided on Agriculture, and intends to return to his native haunts to tell the inhabitants how to make two blades of grass grow where the bullfrog formerly reigned supreme.

name: Abbeville, B. E.
motto: A big, burly, blushing boy
description: “Ben,” or “Toots,” as some call him, is a big blushing boy from McCormick, and is almost the most modest young man in the class. “Ben” couldn’t be induced to walk through one of the big department stores in Atlanta—so you see he must be a modest youth. 'Tis said that he is “coming out,” though; and now boasts of a few admirers in that wild mountain city, Westminster, where people are still in the barbaric state. Everyone knows this boy, because great has been his career as a football “hero.”

name: Bull, Paul
motto: Let the world turn round
description: Paul came to Clemson in the fall of '09, with a banjo in one hand and 'determination to succeed' in the other. His banjo solved the rat problem; but since that time he has had another to disturb him, namely the inspection of the commandant, before ten o'clock every night. Paul is a ladies' man, in deed and in truth, as is attested by his frequent visits to a nearby college (male, of course). Always cheerful and gay, he is one who never lets his troubles worry him.

name: Bull, Brix
motto: Speed! the call of the times is for speed.
description: “Brix” hails at present from the state of Florida, though he once called Columbia his home. We don’t know what he was chased for, and he is himself reticent on that point. However, we do know, my friends, that he plays everything there is to play, and does it well. When it comes to baseball, football, and outguessing the professors, “Pony” is there with the goods. He is also some Paderewski at leading a band. We predict that unless he dies young he will some day compete in the Olympic games at Pekin, China.

name: Bull, Thomas
motto: Our town, our whole town, and nothing but our town
clubs: Treasurer S. C. I. O. A.
description: Corporal, First Sergeant, Lieutenant. 'PREP,' or 'TOMMY,' hails from the famous city of Killian; though, when asked where he lives, claims Columbia. On account of faithful discharge of his duties as First Sergeant, 'PREP' was promoted to a First Lieutenantcy. His chief amusement is sending pillow-covers to girls for future payments in candy. 'TOMMY' also likes to visit the store every vacant hour, for a 'beef,' and to have 'Marshmallow Dainties' every night. His ambition is to be a prosperous dairy farmer at Killian, and have a lady from a nearby college as his better half.

name: Anderson, C. J.
motto: Rate: One dollar per year
description: Here is the champion Tiger-buster of the age. 'Kirk' took charge of the 'Tiger' when that animal was in a most debilitated condition; and look at it now—the leading weekly of the college! So we see that, besides being some 'hobo'—not to mention soldierly qualities—we have some literary guy. New York won't be in it when he lays Honeya Path out on a scientific basis, and puts in street cars.

name: Spartanburg, James Orton
motto: Give every man thy ear, but few thy voice
clubs: Corporal, First Sergeant, Regimental Sergeant - Major, Captain
Vice-President and Secretary S. C. I. Tennis Association
Scrub Baseball '10, Y. M. C. A. Cabinet '12 (member every year)
Southern Intercollegiate Champion, Doubles and Singles, in Tennis.
description: This long, tall bean is known as "Big Sook" by all. Since entering college, he has taken an active part in athletics, winning State-wide reputation as a basket-ball tosser and a welder of the racket. He has never been in danger of a nervous breakdown due to excessive study, though he stands well in his classes. "Sook's" ability to take things as they come is one of his most notable characteristics.

name: Evans, Andrew Brown
motto: Architecture is frozen music; Therefore I'm a composer
description: "Andy" hails from the "City by the Sea"—needless to say, if you ever heard him talk. "B. P." has two aims in life—one the building of a house at Skyland, N. C., and the other a short route to the postoffice. As a sideline to architecture, "COLLEGE" pursues basket-ball; he says it shows off his build. "COLLEGE," when he becomes rich, will marry, go into partnership with Dr. Steinmetz as manager of the Westinghouse Electric Company, and live at Skyland.

name: Craw, Andrew Julius
motto: Confound, I'm a cuspiter
clubs: Vice-President, Recording Secretary Palmetto Literary Society
description: Since coming to Clemson, this little man has accomplished many wonderful things—in his own opinion. Allowed free rein, he will give a lucid account of how he did it. 'Rastrus' is also some debater, it being a common thing for him to keep the air hot for two or three days and a night over a disputed point with his roommate. 'Rastrus' holds a high place in the regard of his friends, and some day will undoubtedly attain great success as an engineer.

name: Anderson
motto: Good old world at that, though
clubs: Member of Staffs of Taps and Tiger, and Exchange Editor Chronicle
Corresponding Secretary, Vice-President, Orator in Annual Celebration
Executive Commissioner S. C. I. O. A.
description: This is 'That Dirty Faint Boy.' Has also been called 'Tut' and 'Boy.' Author of the popular novel, 'The Man Hyer Up.' The scene is laid around Winthrop, and the story is tragedy of the highest order. Chronic optimism is 'Tut's' chief asset, and this is one of the reasons he is deservedly popular with both sexes. Wallace, King of Scotland, had his kingdom saved by a spider; but alas, this man's kingdom was destroyed by a kitten.

name: Hobo
motto: D. D. C. or nothing
description: "Fitz" is another one of those poor creatures who were slighted by the military department. He seems to have never given up hope, however, for he is always handling his gun, even on Saturdays. One who is not a friend of "Fitz's" is not true to himself. His generous disposition and manly ways have given him friends who will watch with interest his promising career.

name: Biddy, Birch
motto: Look, he's winding up the watch of his wit; by and by it will strike
description: The 'City by the Sea' is responsible for this prodigy. 'Biddy,' sometimes known as 'Birch,' has sent his application to the authorities in New York for the position now held by the Goddess of Liberty—enlightening the world, because the brilliant rays of light which emanate from his head dazzle the country for miles around. He is a typical Charlestonian, possessing the broque; and he is a jolly good fellow, with a well-developed sense of humor. To become an M.D. is the height of his ambition.

name: Smith, Peter
motto: For if his name be George, we will call him 'Peter,' for in newborn honors names are forgot
description: "PETER" is a product of Lexington County, and received his early education at Lexington. Being of a studious nature, and desirous of becoming a 'Factoryman,' he came to Clemson. "PETER" has a melodious, frog-like voice, and he exercises in such airs as 'Put Your Arms Around Me, Honey,' etc. "PETER" is fond of reading such books as 'Cute Fairy' stories. His brightest aspiration is to some day become a famous textile man. 'PETER' is a great orator, often addressing Sunday Schools during vacations.

name: Clarendon, Thomas
motto: Not that I love military honors less, but my friends more
description: RED blew in from Sumerton, for the purpose of equipping himself to be a famous engineer. He never gets crossed... And this accounts for his giving up his early ambition to revolutionize the electrical industry. His friends took charge of his jolly good nature right on the jump, and have left him little time to ponder over vague theories and such. Give him love, friends, and fudge, and the cares of life dare not trespass. He cherishes fond hopes of living near Allen-dale.

name: Bill
motto: They toil not; neither do they spin
description: In the fall of '09, 'Bill' informed the inhabitants of Santuc that they would have to get somebody else to run the town, as he was leaving for Clemson. The conductor put him off at Calhoun, and he soon became famous for his literary ability. 'Santuc' spends all his vacant time rereading his letters from 'Jack.' He will become famous as the author of three books, namely: 'How I Won My Shoulder-Straps,' 'My Jack,' and 'Santuc—Past, Present, and Future.'

name: Shine, Hobart
motto: Let your light so shine
clubs: President and Recording Secretary Calhoun Literary Society
description: Everyone knows 'Shine,' and likes him. He can be recognized a block by his erect, military carriage. How did he get his name? Why, he is a son that shines, and whenever he takes a 'shine' on anything he generally makes it shine, so we see he is a shining light. Besides 'hobing,' 'SHINE'S' chief work is writing love letters for the Chronicle, though very few of them are ever returned by the fair critic at a certain female college. name: Charleston, P. C.
motto: She who is worth wooing is worth winning
description: "P. C." came to Clemson in the fall of 1907, to become an engineer. After a year he decided that he could do better as a farmer, so he changed to Agriculture. In the Spring of '08, "P. C." took a stroll over to Pendleton, which gained him a free trip home. After many attempts, he landed in the mail pouch in 1912. "P. C." hates to go to church on Sunday; he would rather go to Seneca. We prophesy that he will be the man-of-all-work on a prosperous Mount Pleasant farm.

name: Hall, James Boyce
motto: Any fool can go to bed; but it takes a man to get up
description: Among the many famous men who claim Central as their home, "J. D." stands pre-eminent. Having learned everything that his school had to offer, he hurried off to Clemson. He arrived just in time for reveille, which he has never missed since. At first, he was contented with instructing the commandant in the Regulations, but owing to "Crip's" persuasive influence, his highest ambition is now to become a corporal in the Salvation Army.

name: Haney, Joseph
motto: All things come to those who wait
description: Just where to place 'this,' scientists cannot agree, so they placed it under the sub-species 'Question Markacease,' temporarily. 'HANNEY' is a typical low-countryman, who proudly claims Johns Island as his roost. He entered Clemson with the class of '12, and found himself in the right church but the wrong pew, and so changed to '13, where he has made fine progress. 'HANAHAN' will some day be cotton mill inspector of South Carolina.

name: Harson, S.
motto: The bees and I are crazy about flowers
description: "Sammie," "Miss Harson," "Old Lady," or "Wreck," of which the latter seems most appropriate in addressing him whose physiognomy adorns this page. "Sis" hails from a brier-patch near Simp-sonville, which accounts for his verdant looks and proverbial fondness for roots and bulbs. He is very modest; nevertheless, he is a heart-smasher. He was so successful last Summer that he cornered a "Beef Trust" from Pickens." We predict for "Sis" a successful career as a mill superintendent and financier.

name: Newberry
motto: Back to the soil
clubs: Recording Secretary, President Calhoun Literary Society
description: "PREACHER" hails from the height of Prosperity; and the wonder is, he doesn't mind admitting it. Nutty? No; because what it takes to be smart, "PREACHER" has it. He is some literary society man, publication man, and soldier. Any man who can rise from a lieutenancy in "M" Company to the captaincy of "A" Company in one year must be a good one. Well, we just guess! He has decided to return to his native city, and become a successful and prosperous farmer.

name: Lee, Blossom
motto: Consider the postage stamp, my son; Its usefulness consists in its ability to stick to one thing till it gets there.
description: "Blossom" cast his lot with the electricals, and has been true to that section ever since. He is also one of the four famous veterans of the Pendleton Guards left at Clemson. Good-natured, and attending to his own affairs, "Blossom" has gone through college with all the friends he needs. Being a persistent man, as well as one of ability, we can predict boundless success as an electrical engineer.

name: Hampton, L.
motto: Truth is a gem of great price.
description: This is the man who put 'Hires' in 'root-beer,' although he wanted to run a Coca-Cola bottling plant in Hampton in his Sophomore year. 'Laurie' is famous for three things, namely: his ability to handle the fair sex; his ball playing; his 'bull' fighting. On account of his religion, 'Laurie' was made Cla's Chaplain. He has never failed at but one thing and that was in trying to prove to the President of a near-by college that he had a cousin there.

name: Greenwood, C. E.
motto: And when he spoke the whole world stopped to listen
description: Greenwood is proud of 'Flip'; so is Clemson. He is sole proprietor of a famous 'German Band,' which has been heard and enjoyed by his many friends. This prodigy is of a very retiring nature, but expects to become a great chemist some day. His contribution to literature while at Clemson comprises three letters to the fair sex. He made his debut as an orator when he addressed the Agricultural Society this year. 'Flip' firmly believes that 'Brevity is the soul of wit'; therefore he is brief.

name: Aiken, Samuel S.
motto: See the conquering hero comes
description: "LOUIE" decided to honor Clemson four years ago, and though at times the storms have battered him, he always shows a "white card" at the finish. The last survivor of the famous "Mutt and Jeff" Hutson Brothers' Dancing School still does himself proud as an adept at the graceful art. From a verdant Fresh, he has seen varied military service. He loves his little reveille nap, and was never known to be in a hurry. We predict a bright future in his chosen profession.

name: Butch
motto: What's in a name? A rose by any other name would smell as sweet.
description: "Benson," old sorrel-top, strolled up from the Governor's county. "Butch" is one of the trio--"Coley," "Rastus," and "Butcher"--who think that there is no other county in the State. "Bessie" is always happy, and never has a serious thought when the guard-room system is working, and he is winning. "Butch" deserted the Lutherans at Newberry, and entered Soph. in '10. Since then he has been making up freehand drawing. He has great ability to stick, and if given time will become a second Edison.

name: Maynard, J. Hans.
motto: Work like a man and don't watch the clock or hang on the whistle cord.
clubs: Treasurer Executive Committee Southern College Press Association
description: “Johnny,” or “Hans,” is from the “Botry,” as his speech shows—unmistakably. When anyone says “Old mossback Charleston” around him, battle signs are hung out immediately, for like all loyal Charlestonians he stands ready to defend her name. His superfluous energy makes all his undertakings successful, so that it is safe to predict that twenty years from now he will be Chief Engineer of the General Electric Company, or “bossing” the electrical end of “Uncle Sam’s” Navy Yards.

name: Hudlam, Skeet
motto: I dinna ken a fe; I am a guid mon
description: Skeet, Hudlam, or Dickie, hails from Greenwood, and he is. Skeet is a great sportsman. Birds being his favorite game. But it's too bad that he hunts out of season. The Bird has flown, and until aeroplanes are made to fly faster, Skeet's aching heart will only be eased by the watch charm which reminds him of another one at whom Dan Cupid use to shoot his arrows. Skeet has many friends. He has a habit of becoming very popular with all whom he knows.

name: Chalmers, J. King
motto: O time! thou must untangle this; not I: It is too hard a knot for me to untie
clubs: Member of Staff Taps and Tiger
description: Of the House of the Red Rose, this monarch is a representative member, being one of the few kings whose hair is a slight shade of the insignia of his House. Not unlike Henry VIII, there are many of the fair sex who appeal to King Chalmers, but he finds it necessary to choose one, since he can't divorce, exile, or behead the extras. 'Exceeding wise, fair spoken, and persuading,' we look to see Chalmers someday Chief Chemist to the Lancastrians.

name: Lamb, Hamilton
motto: Once a friend, always a friend
description: This modest little king was a great addition to our class when he joined it in 1912 from Auburn—in quality, however; not quantity. He is highly esteemed by all who know him, for his gentlemanly and courteous manners. There is some attraction other than home that very often makes him homesick, and he always takes it out in sending an epistle down toward Jacksonville. We predict a bright future for him.

name: LEXINGTON, Bull
motto: My true love hath my heart, and I have his ???
description: In September, 1909, "Bill," became afflicted with hay(fes) fever, and came to Clemson for treatment. One glance at him, and one can tell he is a sport. It is said that once, while working in a dairy, "BILL" had such a severe attack of "feminitis" that his life was only saved by his making love to a cow. Since getting his Senior privileges, he often wanders into the jungles of Calhoun, and has several times been seen going down the 'pike' with a "pain" in his arms.

name: Frank
motto: How can ye chant, ye little birds, An' I see fu' o' cares?
description: FRANK, or BUDDIE, after completing his course at O. I. C., decided to carry his military prowess into larger fields, so he landed at Clemson in 1909. It is said that Frank was once in love, but we have every reason to doubt it; nevertheless, he is very fond of writing love letters and stories. Frank's intellectual ability has never been doubted, high marks being his hobby. His future is somewhat uncertain, but it is thought that he will either be commandant at O. C. or president of the Orangeburg Solomonator Company.

name: Barwell, F. R.
motto: A man's a fool if not taught in woman's school!
description: "Mac" was once a farmer lad, but he couldn't understand why they used two wires on a telephone line so he came to Clemson to find out. "Great has been his find thereof" (also his fines). He was one of the best football players Clemson ever produced—as manager. We have hopes that "Mac" will startle the world with an all-night light that can't be discovered; but he says "Aw, naw, the simple life for muh!" "Sam" and "Crip" will be succeeded by my old mule "Mike."

name: Magill, Rummy
motto: Let us eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow we may die.
description: This is 'Rummy' Magill, from Abbeville. How he got his name is a secret; so ask no questions. 'RUMMY' intended to enter the Olympic games at Stockholm last year as a sprinter, as he is very fast; but he fell and broke his leg. However, the accident didn't hurt his other accomplishments, to wit: 'turkeytrotting' at early hours, sleeping, and eating Hershey Chocolate. We venture to say that 'Dear James' will some day be the county demonstrator of Abbeville County, if he doesn't take to the fishing industry in Georgetown.

name: McAlhany, Thomas Samuel
motto: Behold a million near. A cashier in a bank
description: Even the town of St. George could produce only one 'Tom' McAlhany, nor was she large enough to hold this spirit at home. In the fall of '09, he escaped and came to Clemson, where he has made a record as a soldier, economist, and 'hobo.' 'Mac' sings, talks, and plays a great deal, in spite of which fact his report is always with the best in his section. 'Mac' will become a civil engineer of distinction, and reflect much credit on his native town, and his instructors.

name: Brown, Maggie
motto: No bird—but an invisible thing, A voice, a mystery
description: In a 'bee-line' from Honea Path came this busy little bee to our 'blooming' Clemson, in search of the honey of knowledge. 'MAGGIE' delights in repairing broken-down automobiles, and is an infant prodigy when it comes to electricity. 'BROWNIE' entered college a little boy, leaves it as a little man; but in years to come he will make great and large men—aye, even Dr. Lammie—to open their eyes in wonder at his achievements.

name: Darlington, William
motto: Thy modesty's a wonder to thy merit!
clubs: Orator Annual Contest: Chaplain, Recording Critic, Recording Secretary
description: “Mac” was first known by his pompadour, and rosy cheeks, and his “Greenness”; later, by his ability to bluff the professors into giving him good marks. He has a quiet disposition, and is a good companion, never leading his friends astray. He is longing for the time to come when he will be—not his own man, but somebody’s else; and be “centrally” located. He will be a credit to the class and to Clemson.

name: Mullett, F. T.
motto: A little more sleep, I heard him complaining; You have waked me too soon, I must clumber again
description: Well, this is "Mullett," from Mullins. His good-natured smile, and his general appearance, have won for him many friends. His favorite pastimes are sleeping—and, sleeping some more. On account of his roving disposition, acquired in early life, he was retired from military duty in February. He is most gallant as a ladies man, and his heroic deeds "cover Dixie like the dew" (ask him about calling the elevator), and at present he is making a conquest at G. F. C.

motto: Well, sir; well, sir; the study of history is the study of humanity
description: "Ed" or "JoJo," as he is better known, hails from the bustling town of Chester. In the fall of '09, he decided to come to Clemson, and join the Class of '13. His favorite pastime is sleeping and eating. During the summer of '11, his ambitions took a turn which almost resulted in serious trouble for him with the postoffice authorities at Charlotte. "Jo" intends to return to the "better town," and convince the people that electric lighting is now a safe means of illumination.

name: Myers, Peter Evans
motto: Drink to me only with thine eyes
description: Corporal, First Sergeant, Lieutenant, Captain. 'Peter,' 'P. E.,' or 'Hevons,' as he is called by the fair sex, decided that he wanted to know more about farming, so he migrated to Clemson in '09. He contracted military aspirations, finally reaching the rank of Captain. During his Senior year, he became an ardent admirer of the female part of the Methodist Church. 'Peter' has done well in his class, and will become a prosperous farmer unless he is struck by 'white lightning,' and goes floating down 'Green River' with 'Paul Jones.'

name: Chesterfield, A. I. E.
motto: Smile, damn you, smile
description: What this lad intends to do, very few seem to know. He is taking the Electrical course, and is good at all of his work—whenever he works. 'Sunny Jim' is a product of the sand-hill region of Chesterfield County, and he stands out pre-eminently as the wit of the class. He was the organizer of the S. P. R. Society, and is a staunch advocate of all its laws. He never worries his room-mate by keeping the lights on too late, or by getting up too early for reveille.

name: Park, Andrew David
motto: I must be akin to the bees; I love carnations so
description: Sleeping Winnsboro awaits, with eager expectation, the return of "Peek" Park. He was the other boy who peeped through a knothole when Lord Cornwallis made his memorable march through the historic old town; but he's not very old. "Peek" stands well in his class, and would have done better but for his enormous correspondence. We cannot but predict for Winnsboro a thorough awakening when "Peek" becomes its city engineer.

name: Sease, Pat
motto: How my heart despised reproof
hometown: Grassy Pond, near Possum Trot School, in Cherokee County
description: From Grassy Pond, near Possum Trot School, in Cherokee County, this worthy youth came to enter his college career as a loyal subject of 'King Sease.' 'PAT' appreciates the privilege of bearing punishment for his fellow-man. The only evidence we have of his ladiesmanship is his letter writing. We expect some day to see 'PAT' Mayor of Grassy Pond, or Superintendent of Possum Trot School.

motto: My destiny is linked with the realities of earth

name: Parson, John Franklin
motto: In arguing, too, the Parson owned his skill; For e'en though vanquished he could argue still.
description: This smiling youth hails from Orangeburg. He came to Clemson, in '09, with the full intention of astonishing the world, and with this end in view took the electrical course. "JOHNIE" is some ladies' man, and many a fair maiden has spent sleepless hours worrying over the face that adorns this page. It is rumored that he found a "Bean" during Fair Week upon whom he was willing to spend his Christmas money; but to his disappointment he found that he had bought a ticket to the "Sucker's Association."

name: Runt, Robert Edgar
motto: Accomplishment is the fruit of persistence
description: Ladies and gentlemen, allow me to introduce to you the only original 'Dignified Senior Textile.' He intends to fit himself for some great work in textile lines, such as running a distillery, as a side line, in the dyeing lab. 'Runt' is a military genius, but this fact has never been discovered by the Commandant. He is especially fond of pretty girls; a professional heart-breaker; and the Lord Chesterfield of 'Winkerville.' 'Runt' is an exceptionally diligent student, and has a good record.

name: Lee, J. W.
motto: Worth makes the man
description: "Mary Jane," "Mary," etc., so called because of her numerous love affairs, joined our ranks as a diminutive rodent, but has rapidly grown, because of "balanced rations." He has always been an industrious lad, and his high ambitions, coupled with his great amount of aggressiveness, have gained for him an enviable record. By his friendly disposition, he has made many friends. We can only bespeak for him success as a producer of "bald-headed" cabbage and "cross-eyed" potatoes.

name: Prewitt, Earl J.
motto: An excellent scholar; yet the worst disorganizer in existence.
clubs: Sergeant-at-Arms, Recording Critic, Treasurer, Columbian Literary Society
description: Prey came to Clemson, and started out by being good. He succeeded wonderfully well, but found it awfully lonesome. A change came over him, and he commenced to get educated. A great future is in store for this genius, and if he lives long enough he'll invent perpetual motion, though he will have to escape being put to death on account of too much affection towards the 'Weaker Sex.'

name: Greenville
motto: Nothing is mine alone; but mine to use, to enjoy, and to share with my fellowmen
clubs: Staff Taps: Chaplain, Corresponding Secretary, Calliope
Literary Society: Y. M. C. A.
description: Surely 'Shorty's' motto is appropriate, since we have known him, and to see him is to like him, and to talk with him is to gain a friend. Since he has been in school, his chief occupation has been the dispensing of what was his, and in this he has won a deserved success. He is a tireless worker, but his avocation seems to be principally the writing of business(?) letters to Winthrop—probably for the 'Chronicle.' We trust that his future is not to be compared to his stature.

name: Charleston, R.
motto: A man's a man for a' that
description: "Rus" hails the "Botty" as his brief-patch. He put two baseballs, a bat, and a glove in his suitcase several years ago, and stole to Clemson. Since then, notwithstanding errors, he has made more than one hit, though it is not recorded that he has stolen anything beside bases. "HEINE" says that after he gets his "pink slip" in June, he is going to make a home run to the "Great White Way," and manage the "Giants."

name: Robinson, Ray
description: Soon after Greers was put on the map, "Roy" contracted Clemsonitis, with the usual results. Early in his "rathood" he established a "rep" as a veritable encyclopedia on love affairs, and won the record for visiting a nearby female college. The time not given to his books is devoted to billet-doux, and though a brilliant future awaits him as a textile expert, his real fame will be as an author. He will soon publish the following: "How to Woo and Win," "Military Tyranny," and "Old Love Letters."

name: Greenwood, James
motto: May he kiss whom he pleases. And please whom he kisses
clubs: Corresponding and Recording Secretary, President Calhoun Literary Society
description: Who doesn't know 'Military Jimmie,' 'Bashful Jimmie,' or the fellow who yells in the mess-hall? The title 'Bashful' was given him by the girls of Greers. At a single leap, 'Jimmie' landed at the top of military fame; so high, indeed, that he can easily advertise his 'demoralized water-buckets.' 'Jimmie's' popularity is truly wonderful. But 'The Greatest Moment of My Life,' and 'How I Met It' was that blissful afternoon when 'I Bade Her Farewell.' For further references, see the joke columns of the 'Tiger,' and calendar of 'Taps.'

name: Greenville, E. E.
motto: Electricity is the most marvelous invention of the age
clubs: Executive Committee A. I. E. E.
description: "Monkey" swung in, in '09, with the rest of them, and has been making things hum ever since. "Sam" and "Crip" are hobbies of his, and his marks soar around a hundred and ten as an average. His military ambition was realized near the end of his career, when he pulled down a high-ranking second "loo." and the way he swings a blade is a revelation. If he doesn't become a general in the war with Japan, in 1957, he will have charge of the General Electric Company.

name: Smart, Will Love
motto: Fesser, yer goin' ter give us any percents?
description: "Will Love" Smarr. Who will? He says all the girls at Bullock's Creek do. Yes, that's where he's from. Maybe that's the reason he is such a bean at 'bull.' "Cree!" has the world skinned when it comes to percents, and he says that he knows how to keep spiders from pulling little chickens into their dens and eating them. He says his future lies between teaching a kindergarten or hauling water to make dry wells flow again.

name: Laurens, C. E.
motto: Grin, and the world grins with you
description: This lad hails from the County of Laurens; we don't give the town, because he may be like a lot more of us from near somewhere. 'C. E.' has won fame in the class by his stick-to-it-iveness, not to mention his pugilistic accomplishments. He believes in being cheerful, and his ready grin greets one morning, noon, and night; we have heard that he wears it in his sleep. If he keeps along his present line of endeavor, Laurens will boast one of the best stock farms in the State in the years to come.

name: Stoudemire, Edgar
motto: One sip of this will bathe the spirits in delight Beyond the bliss of dreams— Be wise, and taste
description: Studemore, more familiarly known as "Stude," received his preparatory training at Chapin. He entered Clemson College with a determination to master the Civil course, but the hum of the loom soon led him to join the "Factory-men." It can be seen from his dreamy expression that "Stude" is a powerful thinker. His thoughts are located near Newberry, where he will make visits in the near future, no doubt. His greatest ambition is to erect the first cotton mill at Chapin, and to spread the fame of his native town.

name: Special, Emerson
motto: Even though vanquished, he could argue still
description: The town of Seneca is honored by the genius whose physiognomy graces this page. "Joe" is an artist of no mean ability, and he decorated his room, and even drew his own postcards. He is a great student of Civil Engineering, for he "shot" "Hobo" for a hundred on an examination. Since "Joe" became the partner of a certain faculty member, as manager of the Cadet Exchange, and has learned how to get rich quick, we fear that he will go into the General Merchandise business.

name: Aiken, Bush
motto: I don't mind demerits, but I hate confinements
description: "Bush," as his name would indicate, comes from a place known as Beach Island, Hawaii at once singled out for a military career, and it took him until the latter part of his Senior year to realize the advantages and honors of a Senior Privilege. His splendid disposition and modest traits have won for him a host of friends, who expect great things of him.

name: Prep
motto: Brevity is a Spartan virtue
description: "Prep" is a worthy product of Gray Court, which has missed him greatly since his departure for what he terms the pen." "Benjamin" has never had any military aspirations, and as a result of seeing no sense in the stuff, he seldom hears that woeful cry of "To-gether, together, together!" He is the same old '66 (Lewis) all the time, and his quiet manner has made him many friends. In fact, he is rather too friendly with some people; for instance, our Commissary-General. We predict for "Truck" a very successful career as a green grocer in his native town.

name: Barnwell, C. E.
motto: His chief virtue an unmitigated patience
description: Truly the 'sticks' doth oft engender the wisest of men, for who knows but that we have within this young Englishman the making of a future Surgeon-General of the Navy? 'Cor-REN,' 'of manners gentle, of affections mild,' carries in his veins no blood of that Carolina governor whose surname, with but slight change, he bears. Not a D.D. nor yet a Ph.D. has the ambition to be. What we predict for him is a successful career as Youmans, M.D., mender of men and render of purse.
